name: Yam

pros.title: useful fibre for a starch-heavy root
pros.explanation: That helps yam separate from lower-fibre carb staples in the category.

pros.title: brings a decent potassium return
pros.explanation: Not elite in every nutrient lane, but clearly better than empty-starch options if the core profile holds up.

pros.title: solid whole-food staple value
pros.explanation: It still offers a cleaner everyday carb package than heavily refined or heavily processed alternatives.

cons.title: nutrition can vary a lot depending on the exact yam
cons.explanation: Yam data is often confused with sweet potato or with different yam species, so the profile needs careful interpretation.

cons.title: micronutrient return is not automatically elite
cons.explanation: It can be a solid root, but it should not be treated like a micronutrient monster without stronger source support.

cons.title: it is still mainly an energy food
cons.explanation: That keeps a ceiling on how high it should climb unless the fibre and micronutrient profile clearly separate it.

sourceNotes: Expanded dataset sample for FoodRanked pressure-testing.
Values are approximate representative placeholders for category tuning, not a clinical nutrient database.
